tools.name: minerva_pcb_set_board_size
tools.description: Set the PCB board dimensions.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_get_components
tools.description: Get all components from a PCB editor with their positions and connections.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_get_nets
tools.description: Get all electrical nets (connections) from a PCB.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_get_pin_position
tools.description: Get the world position and info for a specific pin on a component. Useful for calculating waypoints or verifying pin locations before creating route hints.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_pin_info
tools.description: Pin inspector parity tool (WC-1): returns the SAME info the panel's Pin Info section shows for a pin — footprint geometry pin name, net, net members, and touching traces — plus display_name computed by the exact display rule the UI uses (geometry pin_name > net > "(unconnected)"). Identify the pin either by ref ("U1.15") or by a board point (x_mm/y_mm, hit-tests the nearest pad).

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

ref.description: Component.Pin reference, e.g. 'U1.15'. Mutually exclusive with x_mm/y_mm.

x_mm.description: Board X in mm — hit-tests the nearest pad. Requires y_mm. Mutually exclusive with ref.

y_mm.description: Board Y in mm. Requires x_mm.

tools.name: minerva_pcb_add_component
tools.description: Add a new component to the PCB. NOTE: Component dimensions are estimated defaults based on footprint type. For accurate sizing from KiCAD libraries, run pcb-architect's footprint-geometry command and then call minerva_pcb_import_footprint_geometry to update components with real dimensions.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

id.description: Component ID (e.g., 'R15', 'U3'). Auto-generated if not specified.

footprint.description: Footprint type: RESISTOR, CAPACITOR, IC_DIP, IC_QFP, SWITCH, CONNECTOR, LED, DIODE, TRANSISTOR, HEADER, MOUNTING_HOLE, MODULE

pin_count.description: Number of pins. Works for all footprint types. HEADER/CONNECTOR = single row, IC_DIP/MODULE = dual row (even), others use generic layout via pad_type/pad_spacing/row_spacing.

pad_type.description: Pad type for placeholder geometry (default: tht). Used when pin_count is set on non-specialised footprint types.

pad_spacing.description: Centre-to-centre pad spacing in mm (default: 2.54). Used with generic pin layout.

row_spacing.description: Row-to-row spacing for dual-row layouts in mm (default: 7.62). Used with generic pin layout.

pin_names.description: Custom pin names (e.g., ['GND', 'VCC', 'SDA', 'SCL'] for a 4-pin header)

snap_to_grid.description: Whether to snap position to grid (default: true). Set to false for exact positioning.

tools.name: minerva_pcb_move_component
tools.description: Move a component to an absolute position. Requires editor_name from minerva_list_editors and component names from minerva_pcb_get_components.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_move_relative
tools.description: Move a component using natural language direction (e.g., 'down a bit', 'closer to U3').

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

direction.description: Natural language direction: 'up', 'down', 'left', 'right', 'closer to X', 'away from X', 'toward center', etc.

tools.name: minerva_pcb_rotate_component
tools.description: Rotate a component. Positive degrees rotate counter-clockwise (CCW), negative rotate clockwise (CW). Requires editor_name from minerva_list_editors.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

degrees.description: Rotation angle. Positive = counter-clockwise (CCW), negative = clockwise (CW). Common values: 90 (CCW), -90 (CW), 180.

tools.name: minerva_pcb_delete_component
tools.description: Delete a component from the PCB. Requires editor_name from minerva_list_editors.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_connect_net
tools.description: Connect component pins to a net (creates net if it doesn't exist). Get pin names from minerva_pcb_describe_component first. Requires editor_name from minerva_list_editors.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_spatial_query
tools.description: Query components based on spatial relationships (e.g., 'what components are near U3?').

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

reference_component.description: Component ID to query relative to

tools.name: minerva_pcb_describe_component
tools.description: Get detailed spatial context for a component including nearby components, connections, and region.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_import_csv
tools.description: Import component placement from CSV.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_export_csv
tools.description: Export PCB component placement as CSV.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_import_footprint_geometry
tools.description: IMPORTANT: Call this after adding components to get accurate dimensions from KiCAD footprint libraries. Without this, components use estimated sizes that may not match actual footprints. Run 'pcb-architect footprint-geometry board.yaml -o geometry.json' to generate the input data. Updates component pads with accurate shapes, sizes, drill holes, and body dimensions. Can also correct positions if YAML used different coordinate conventions (use position_is_center and/or invert_y flags).

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

geometry.description: Footprint geometry JSON from pcb-architect footprint-geometry command. Contains board_name and components with pad arrays.

position_is_center.description: If true, current component positions are geometric centers (not footprint origins). Will adjust positions by subtracting bounding_box center offset. Default: false

invert_y.description: If true, Y coordinates are inverted (Y=0 at bottom instead of top). Will flip Y relative to board height. Default: false

tools.name: minerva_pcb_get_change_journal
tools.description: Get the change journal for a PCB editor. Returns an append-only log of forward actions (moves, rotations, deletions, etc.) with timestamps.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

since_timestamp.description: Optional Unix timestamp to filter entries from. Only entries at or after this time are returned.

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (most recent). Default: 50

tools.name: minerva_pcb_import_trace_geometry
tools.description: Import routed traces and vias from pcb-architect's trace-geometry command output. Clears existing traces and imports new ones. Trace segments are automatically connected into polylines.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

trace_data.description: Trace geometry JSON from pcb-architect trace-geometry command

tools.name: minerva_pcb_export_trace_geometry
tools.description: Export routed traces and vias from a PCB editor. Returns trace data in the same format accepted by import_trace_geometry, enabling round-trip workflows.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

tools.name: minerva_pcb_get_image
tools.description: Export a PCB view as a PNG image. Prefer save_to_path — inline base64 is large and will bloat your context. Without save_to_path, the PNG is returned inline as base64 (image_data), which is expensive and should only be used when the caller specifically needs the bytes in the response.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

width.description: Requested image width in pixels (advisory). Default: 800

height.description: Requested image height in pixels (advisory). Default: 600

save_to_path.description: Optional absolute filesystem path to write the PNG to instead of returning it inline as base64. Parent directory must already exist. When set, the response omits image_data and instead returns {saved_to, width, height, byte_size} — read the file back with your file-read tool if you need to view it.

tools.name: minerva_pcb_apply_route_hints
tools.description: Route the board's open route hints and either PROPOSE routes (default) or COMMIT them as real traces. Default (commit absent/false): runs the router over the selected open route hints and writes the results back as AI-authored (cyan) proposal annotations — inspectable polylines that do NOT mutate the board; each proposal links to the source hint(s) it answers (kind_payload.proposal_for). Set commit=true to materialize the routed polylines as real traces in the board model (journaled) and transition the source hints open→applied. Partial/failed routing returns WHERE it got stuck (unrouted nets with their blocked pad pairs) as structured feedback. Iterate: edit/add hints and re-run; applied hints are excluded by default so only fresh open hints re-route.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

hint_ids.description: Optional explicit route-hint annotation ids to route. Omit to route all OPEN route hints (applied/resolved hints are excluded).

commit.description: false/absent (default): write back cyan proposal annotations only, board unchanged. true: materialize routed traces + mark source hints applied.

tools.name: minerva_pcb_proposal_accept
tools.description: Accept ONE AI-authored route proposal (a cyan pcb_route_hint annotation carrying kind_payload.proposal_for, written back by minerva_pcb_apply_route_hints with commit=false or the panel's Propose button). Materializes ONLY that proposal's polyline as a real trace in the board model (journaled), then deletes the consumed source hint(s) (kind_payload.proposal_for) AND the proposal itself — accepted scaffolding disappears. Shares its materialization logic with the bulk apply (commit=true) path, so the resulting trace geometry is identical either way.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

id.description: The proposal annotation id to accept (must carry kind_payload.proposal_for)

tools.name: minerva_pcb_proposal_reject
tools.description: Reject ONE AI-authored route proposal: deletes the proposal annotation only. The source hint(s) it answered (kind_payload.proposal_for) are left open for further iteration (re-propose, edit, or a different route).

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

id.description: The proposal annotation id to reject (must carry kind_payload.proposal_for)

tools.name: minerva_pcb_hint_undo
tools.description: Undo the most recent tracked edit (waypoints/layer/width_mm/detail_level) to a route hint's per-hint revision stack, restoring its prior kind_payload. Works over BOTH human canvas edits (the Edit-hint bend-drag tool) and prior agent edits (minerva_annotations_update) — every edit path shares one bounded revision stack per hint (cap 25), so this undoes whichever edit landed last regardless of who made it.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

id.description: The pcb_route_hint annotation id to undo

tools.name: minerva_pcb_hint_redo
tools.description: Redo a previously undone edit to a route hint's per-hint revision stack, restoring the kind_payload that undo just reverted. Cleared by any fresh edit made after the undo (standard undo/redo semantics — you cannot redo past a new edit).

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

id.description: The pcb_route_hint annotation id to redo

tools.name: minerva_pcb_add_via
tools.description: Manually insert a via on a pcb_route_hint proposal (a route carrying kind_payload.segments): splits the segment nearest board point (x, y) into two at the projected point, appends a via there, and recomputes every segment's copper layer via the layer-run toggle (F.Cu<->B.Cu at each via position, in path order) — the same split+via+layer-run geometry the canvas Add-Via tool uses, so an agent's call and a human's click produce identical results. Use this to resolve a routing collision by hand when the autorouter's own vias-avoidance left a proposal single-layer through an obstacle: one via jumps the trace to the opposite layer past that point, a second via jumps it back. No-op (error) if (x, y) is not near any segment. Persists through the host's revision-history seam, so minerva_pcb_hint_undo/minerva_pcb_hint_redo cover it.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

id.description: The pcb_route_hint proposal annotation id to add a via to

x.description: Board-space X (mm) of the point to split/via

y.description: Board-space Y (mm) of the point to split/via

tools.name: minerva_pcb_load_board
tools.description: Load a complete PCB board from canonical YAML into the editor in ONE call — components (with pins and pad geometry), nets, traces, vias, and mounting holes. This is the whole-board importer; it replaces the multi-call add_component / connect_net / import_footprint_geometry / import_trace_geometry sequence. The `yaml` is the canonical board contract (width_mm/height_mm, components[].ref whose pins carry pad geometry via drill_mm/annulus_diameter_mm for through-hole or pad_width_mm/pad_height_mm for SMD, plus nets, traces, vias, mounting_holes). Returns component_count, net_count, and any deserialize warnings.

editor_name.description: Name of the PCB editor tab

skills.title: PCB design (migration build — worker compute online)
skills.summary: PCB editor under migration from Minerva core. Opens .pcbskel board documents (JSON), renders a crude component view, supports platform pcb_route_hint annotations with sidecar persistence, and has a Python compute worker: minerva_pcb_validate (board YAML structural + design-rule checks), minerva_pcb_generate (YAML -> .kicad_pcb/.kicad_sch/.kicad_pro text, no KiCad install), minerva_pcb_check_libraries / minerva_pcb_check_bom (real footprint/symbol checks + BOM suggestions against a fetched KiCAD library-data subset; minerva_pcb_fetch_libraries fetches+verifies it, minerva_pcb_library_status reports what's present). pcb.serialize/pcb.deserialize are the canonical-YAML board codec (see docs/board-yaml.md). The real editor canvas, trace routing (agent-router), and gerber export land in later rounds; .minpcb stays with the in-tree editor until cutover.
skills.preconditions: A .pcbskel document is open OR the user has asked for PCB work (an anonymous editor can be spawned via minerva_create_plugin_editor with editor_item_id "new_pcb").
skills.outcome: A .pcbskel board renders in the panel; pcb_route_hint annotations author via the dock and persist to the <file>.annotations.json sidecar; boards validate and generate KiCad files via the worker tools; footprint/symbol/BOM checks run against real KiCAD library data once fetched.
skills.steps:
1. Call minerva_pcb_ping to confirm the backend is alive.
2. Open a .pcbskel file (or create one via editor_item new_pcb).
3. Validate a board with minerva_pcb_validate (canonical YAML per docs/board-yaml.md); generate KiCad files with minerva_pcb_generate.
4. Run minerva_pcb_fetch_libraries once (or check minerva_pcb_library_status) so minerva_pcb_check_libraries/minerva_pcb_check_bom can resolve real footprints instead of returning missing_data.
5. Use the core minerva_annotations_* tools against the panel's editor tab for route-hint work.
6. For anything beyond this (real canvas editing, routing, gerbers), explain it lands in later rounds — the in-tree PCB editor still owns .minpcb.
skills.system_prompt: The PCB plugin is mid-migration from Minerva core. It opens .pcbskel JSON boards, draws components crudely, and supports pcb_route_hint annotations through the platform annotation substrate (dock authoring, sidecar persistence, minerva_annotations_query/render_overlay). Backend: minerva_pcb_ping; canonical-YAML board codec on pcb.serialize/pcb.deserialize; Python worker tools minerva_pcb_validate (structural + design-rule validation), minerva_pcb_generate (KiCad file text generation, pure Python), minerva_pcb_check_libraries and minerva_pcb_check_bom (real footprint/symbol checks + nearest-name BOM suggestions once library data is fetched; missing_data:true with a hint field before that). minerva_pcb_fetch_libraries downloads+sha256-verifies a curated KiCAD library subset (docs/libraries.md); minerva_pcb_library_status reports fetch state without fetching. NO trace routing or gerber export yet; .minpcb documents still open in the in-tree PCB editor, not this plugin.
